# How to Distribute Your Generated CLI

This guide covers building, packaging, and distributing CLI applications
generated by Cliford.

## Enabling release artifacts

```bash
cliford generate --release
```

Or in `cliford.yaml`:

```yaml
features:
  distribution:
    goreleaser: true
    homebrew: false       # Set true to generate a Homebrew formula
```

## Generated files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.goreleaser.yaml` | Cross-platform build configuration |
| `.github/workflows/release.yaml` | GitHub Actions workflow (triggers on `v*` tags) |
| `install.sh` | Unix install script (macOS/Linux) |
| `install.ps1` | Windows install script |
| `homebrew/<app>.rb` | Homebrew formula template |

The `owner` field in `.goreleaser.yaml` and the repository URLs in install
scripts are set to the app name by default. You can override this with the
`SetOwner` API on each distribution generator, or by editing the generated
files directly.

## How to create a release

### Tag and push

```bash
git tag v1.0.0
git push origin v1.0.0
```

The GitHub Actions workflow runs automatically on version tags.

### Build locally

```bash
goreleaser check                       # Validate config
goreleaser build --snapshot --clean     # Build without publishing
goreleaser release --clean             # Build and publish
```

### Platforms and architectures

The generated `.goreleaser.yaml` produces binaries for:

- **OS**: Linux, macOS (Darwin), Windows
- **Arch**: amd64, arm64
- **Format**: `.tar.gz` (Unix), `.zip` (Windows)
- **Checksums**: SHA256 in `checksums.txt`

## Version injection

Version, commit hash, build date, and app title are injected via ldflags:

```yaml
ldflags:
  - -X main.version={{.Version}}
  - -X main.commit={{.Commit}}
  - -X main.date={{.Date}}
  - -X main.appTitle=<app-name>
```

The generated app displays this information with `--version`:

```
myapp version 1.2.3 (commit: abc1234, built: 2026-04-10T12:00:00Z)
```

## How to set up install scripts

### Unix (install.sh)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/myorg/myapp/main/install.sh | sh
```

The script detects OS and architecture, fetches the latest release from
GitHub, verifies the SHA256 checksum, and installs to `/usr/local/bin`.

### Windows (install.ps1)

```powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/myorg/myapp/main/install.ps1 | iex
```

Installs to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\<app>\` and adds it to the user PATH.

## How to set up Homebrew

The generated formula in `homebrew/<app>.rb` is a template. To publish:

1. Create a tap repository: `myorg/homebrew-tap`
2. Copy the formula file to `Formula/<app>.rb`
3. Update SHA256 hashes and version after each release

Users then install with:

```bash
brew tap myorg/tap
brew install <app>
```

## Version management

### SemVer

Generated apps use semantic versioning. The version is set in `cliford.yaml`
and injected at build time via ldflags.

### Version bumping

```bash
cliford version patch     # 1.2.3 -> 1.2.4
cliford version minor     # 1.2.3 -> 1.3.0
cliford version major     # 1.2.3 -> 2.0.0
cliford version auto      # Analyze spec changes, pick automatically
```

Auto-bump logic:
- Operations added: minor bump
- Operations removed or signatures changed: major bump
- Descriptions or metadata only: patch bump

## How to generate documentation

The generated app includes a `generate-docs` subcommand:

```bash
# Generate man pages
./myapp generate-docs --format man --output-dir ./docs/man

# Generate Markdown documentation
./myapp generate-docs --format markdown --output-dir ./docs/md
```

Cliford also generates documentation at generation time:

| Output | Location | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Markdown CLI reference | `docs/cli/index.md` | Command index with all operations |
| Per-tag docs | `docs/cli/<tag>.md` | Operations with flags, auth, HTTP details |
| LLM-optimized docs | `docs/llms.txt` | Flat text for AI agent context windows |

Shell completions are available via the built-in `completion` command:

```bash
./myapp completion bash
./myapp completion zsh
./myapp completion fish
```
